clinical_history: Which of the following statements about this ECG from a 23-yr-old woman with chest pain is correct?
diagnosis: a) The ECG shows sinus rhythm at a rate of about 60/min and is completely within normal limits. The electrical axis (about +50 degrees) and basic intervals (PR, QRS and QT) are all normal. The P waves are normal. The QRS complexes have a normal morphology. The precordial leads show normal R wave progression (transition zone between V3 and V4). The ventricular repolarization (ST-T complex) is physiologic (with a normal QT interval) making severe hypokalemia or hypocalcemia unlikely. Severe hypokalemia generally causes repolarization (QT-U) prolongation (usually with flat T waves and sometimes ST sagging). Severe hypocalcemia prolongs the plateau phase of the ventricular action potential, associated QT prolongation due to a stretched out ST segment.
